channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump’s crypto reserve is a payoff for loyalists
item.description: President Donald Trump has found a new way to reward his supporters. On Sunday, Trump announced in a Truth Social post that he will issue an executive order creating a “Crypto Strategic Reserve” that will include Bitcoin, Ethereum, and three other cryptocurrencies. “I will make sure the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: You made this happen: a giant Lego Steamboat that we all want to build
item.description: While Lego has made all sorts of vessels – a massive model of the Titanic and a submarine included – this is the brand's first steamboat. The Lego Ideas River Steamboat was unveiled alongside a few other sets at the 2025 Toy Fair.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: AI Chatbots have telltale quirks. Researchers can spot them with 97% accuracy
item.description: There’s a cat-and-mouse game between those using generative AI chatbots to produce text undetected and those trying to catch them. Many believe they know the telltale signs—though as a journalist fond of the word “delve” and prone to em-dashes, I’m not so sure. Researchers at four U.S.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Google is working on its own version of Apple’s Hide My Email, and you might soon be able to try it yourself
item.description: Google is reportedly working on a new Shielded Email feature that will hide your email address when signing up for accounts, similar to the existing Hide My Email feature offered by Apple.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: This is the most powerful rugged smartphone ever built, and its CPU is supposedly faster than the iPhone 15 Pro Max
item.description: Ulefone’s Armor 28 Ultra brings flagship power to rugged phones with a Dimensity 9300+ chip and AI capabilities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Mozilla is under fire for its updated Firefox user agreement
item.description: Mozilla last week updated the Firefox user agreement — something that normally does not provoke strong reactions. This time, however, the changes led to a wave of criticism, because some of the wording can be interpreted as giving Mozilla free rein to do whatever it wants with users’ data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US Cybercom, CISA retreat in fight against Russian cyber threats: reports
item.description: Two reports published on Friday signaled the Trump administration may no longer consider Russia the US government’s top cyber foe, a development that would radically alter long-standing American global alliances to defend against nation-state threat actors in the digital realm.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra could have even smaller bezels - and that could mean an even bigger display
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra has been tipped to come with even smaller bezels than the Galaxy S25 Ultra, which is already near enough all screen – that extra shrinkage could mean a 7-inch Galaxy display is on the way.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Bad onboarding is costing businesses billions - here's how you can improve
item.description: A bad employee onboarding experience could be costing US businesses more than they realize thanks to broader knock-on effects.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Lenovo's latest iteration of popular Yoga laptop range comes with exceptional tandem OLED tech
item.description: Lenovo adds two Intel-powered laptops to its Aura Edition Yoga range, and refreshes the AMD-powered Yoga Pro 7.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Java Platform Reaches New Heights with JDK 24
item.description: JDK 24, the latest release of the Java platform, features a record-breaking 24 JDK Enhancement Proposals (JEPs), including significant enhancements to class loading and linking, stream gatherers, security management, virtual threads, and more.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lenovo just launched the lightest AMD Ryzen AI laptop ever; ThinkPad X13 Gen 6 tops scale at just over 2 lbs
item.description: Lenovo has unveiled the ThinkPad X13 Gen 6 which is super-light at just 2 lbs and comes with the latest AMD or Intel AI CPUs.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Energy Department scientists participate in 'AI Jam' with OpenAI, Anthropic
item.description: Nearly 1,000 scientists from multiple U.S. National Laboratories gathered Friday to test artificial intelligence (AI) models from leading firms like OpenAI and Anthropic in an effort to harness the advancing technology for science and national security purposes.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Washington Post loses 75K subscribers after Bezos-ordered op-ed pivot
item.description: The Washington Post has reportedly lost tens of thousands of subscribers in an apparent reaction to a decision by the outlet's billionaire owner to change the editorial focus of its opinion pages.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Lawrence Livermore's El Capitan Named World's Fastest Supercomputer
item.description: The Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory has been named home to the world’s fastest, most powerful supercomputer, El Capitan.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas
item.description: Federal use cases typically fall into one of five “swim lanes,” with most impacting customer or citizen services, said the IDC Insight's federal researcher. The post AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Five takeaways from Cisco Live EMEA
item.description: Cisco held its European user event in Amsterdam this month, presenting a good mix of industry trends, product innovation, and vision from Cisco as to where they see the market going.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Red Hat OpenShift 4.18 expands cloud-native networking
item.description: Red Hat is updating its OpenShift platform with a series of capabilities that will provide more advanced networking and virtualization functionality for cloud-native deployments. OpenShift is Red Hat’s commercially supported Kubernetes distribution.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 11 rules for writing better code
item.description: You can’t develop software and manage software projects for 30 years and not learn a thing or two or twelve about writing good code. Here are a near-dozen nuggets of coding wisdom I picked up along the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World
